question_title: Will the interest in “russia” change between 2026-03-02 and 2026-03-14 according to Google Trends?
summary: Google Trends interest in "russia" starts at 95 in the resolution window (near the ceiling of 100). Historical 12-day changes from elevated starting values show an 83-85% decrease rate (n=12-13). The mean-reverting Monte Carlo simulation (capturing strong negative autocorrelation of -0.459) gives 76% probability of decrease. Previous identical spike patterns (Feb 12-15 at 95-100) decayed to 62 within 10-13 days. The ±3 "Doesn't change" threshold is extremely narrow relative to 12-day volatility (std ~23.5 points) — no 12-day intervals showed less than ±3 change. The Abu Dhabi peace talks in early March are a live catalyst that could sustain interest, but even spike-driven interest historically decays within the forecast window. Olympics-related queries, a major driver, are fading post-Feb 22.

factors.description: Starting value at 95 (near ceiling of 100) with strong mean-reversion pattern: all 12-day changes from high starting values (>=59) showed decreases (83-85% rate, n=12-13). Mean-reverting MC confirms at 76%. This is the dominant signal.

Previous spike pattern (Feb 12-15 at 95-100) decayed to 62 within 10-13 days. Current spike (Feb 28-Mar 2) shows same elevated pattern that historically reverts. Spikes in 'russia' interest are transient.
Olympics-related queries (major driver of recent interest) are fading as Winter Olympics 2026 ended Feb 22. This removes a key pillar of elevated interest, supporting decay.

Abu Dhabi peace talks scheduled 'early March' described as 'decisive' - Russia may walk away if Ukraine doesn't cede territory. This is a live catalyst that could spike or sustain interest during the forecast window.

Extremely narrow ±3 threshold relative to observed 12-day volatility (std ~23.5 points). No 12-day windows in the resolution timeframe showed 'Doesn't change' (0/7). This makes the middle outcome unlikely.
factors.conditional: This factor reduces P(Doesn't change) and redistributes probability to the tails, mainly Decreases given the other factors.

factors.description: SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement differences could be ±2 points, which matters at the ±3 threshold boundary. Adds uncertainty to the exact resolution.

reasoning:
**Forecast: Decreases 67%, Increases 20%, Doesn't change 13%**

Key reasoning:

1. **Starting at 95/100 is the dominant signal.** Every 12-day period starting from elevated values in this series showed a decrease (4/4 from ≥89, extending to 10-11/12-13 from ≥59). The mean-reverting MC model gives 76% decrease probability.

2. **Spike decay pattern is consistent.** The previous Feb 12-15 spike (95-100) decayed to 62 within 10-13 days. The current spike follows the same trajectory.

3. **The ±3 threshold is extremely narrow.** With 12-day volatility of ~23.5 points, "Doesn't change" is very unlikely — zero instances in 7 observed 12-day windows.

4. **Abu Dhabi peace talks are a real catalyst** but likely insufficient to overcome the strong decay signal. Even if talks generate a spike, historical patterns show decay within 7-10 days, and Mar 14 is 7-11 days after the likely talk dates.
